title: Sending parcels between Great Britain and Northern Ireland under the Windsor Framework
body: "Sending correspondence from Great Britain to Northern Ireland  Correspondence sent from Great Britain (England, Scotland and Wales) to Northern Ireland is exempt from any requirements. These include letters, postcards and printed matter.  Sending goods in parcels from Great Britain to Northern Ireland  Arrangements for sending parcels from Great Britain to Northern Ireland depend on the type of parcel you are moving and whether you’re:   a private individual in Great Britain sending a parcel to a private individual in Northern Ireland or returning goods to a business in Northern Ireland a business in Great Britain sending parcels to a private individual in Northern Ireland or a business in Northern Ireland an express operator or another organisation responsible for sending parcels from Great Britain to Northern Ireland   Sending sanitary and phytosanitary goods in parcels from Great Britain to Northern Ireland  In addition to standard requirements, there may be additional requirements when you’re sending sanitary and phytosanitary goods in parcels from Great Britain to Northern Ireland.  Find out how to send sanitary and phytosanitary goods in parcels from Great Britain to Northern Ireland.  Sending excise goods in parcels from Great Britain to Northern Ireland  In addition to standard requirements, excise duty may be payable on the movement of excise goods from Great Britain to Northern Ireland. Businesses sending excise goods in parcels can no longer delay and must account for all excise duty on all movements where you are liable to pay.  Read more about how to account for duty under the excise duty offset mechanism.  Send parcels from Northern Ireland to Great Britain  Qualifying Northern Ireland goods continue to have unfettered access to Great Britain when they are sent in parcels or freight. Unfettered access means that you can move parcels as normal, with no new requirements.  A small number of goods may be categorised as non-qualifying Northern Ireland goods.  In this case, you may need to meet additional customs requirements such as making an entry summary declaration.  Find out more about moving qualifying goods from Northern Ireland to the rest of the UK.  "

taxons.title: Customs declarations, duties and tariffs (import and export)
